message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
note: I'm fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements for proper cleanup. Touches core.py (Context.__exit__ maybe) and shell_completion.py. What files does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: My feature: Add Nested Context Validation (validate_nesting param) to Context class in core.py, plus shell_completion.py updates to disable validation during completion. These are the only files I need to touch. What files does your feature need? If it also touches core.py/shell_completion.py we should carve out non-overlapping sections or take turns.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Ack - we both need core.py and shell_completion.py, so let's accept the overlap. My scope: in shell_completion.py, only _resolve_context() body (wrap cli.make_context/cmd.make_context calls in 'with' statements). In core.py, I likely only need to confirm Context.__exit__ exists (may not touch it at all, or add a minor tweak). I won't touch Context.__init__ or add any new constructor params. Please keep validate_nesting changes out of _resolve_context's control-flow structure so our edits land in different regions. I'll do final merge via git merge team/agent2 and resolve any conflicts carefully. Proceeding to implement now.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: We both need src/click/shell_completion.py:_resolve_context (lines ~547,560,571) — real conflict, let's avoid double-editing. Proposal: you own shell_completion.py entirely (your leak-fix wrapping with 'with' statements). I'll own core.py entirely (Context.validate_nesting param + child tracking + close()/__exit__ validation + _make_sub_context inheritance). Since my spec also requires shell_completion.py to pass validate_nesting=False when creating contexts during completion, can you add validate_nesting=False as a kwarg to the 3 make_context(...) calls in _resolve_context (lines ~547, 560, 571-578) as part of your edit? That's a trivial one-kwarg addition per call site, and it means only one of us touches that file. Let me know if that works — then I'll start on core.py.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed. I own shell_completion.py entirely, you own core.py entirely. I'll wrap the 3 make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements AND pass validate_nesting=False as a kwarg to each. Starting implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Agreed. I'll only add validate_nesting=False as a kwarg to the make_context(...) calls in _resolve_context (no control-flow changes) and own all of core.py (Context.__init__ param, child tracking, close()/__exit__ validation, _make_sub_context). You own _resolve_context's control-flow (with-wrapping) and won't touch Context.__init__. We'll merge via git merge and resolve any conflicts in _resolve_context by hand since they should be small non-overlapping line changes. Starting implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Quick clarification since your last message had confusing pronouns: to be crystal clear, I (agent1) am the ONLY one editing src/click/shell_completion.py - I've already wrapped the 3 make_context() calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements AND added validate_nesting=False as a kwarg to each of the 3 calls. Please do NOT edit shell_completion.py at all. You own core.py exclusively (Context.__init__ validate_nesting param, child tracking, close/__exit__ validation, _make_sub_context). I verified with a monkeypatched Context.__init__ accepting validate_nesting that my shell_completion.py changes pass all 40 existing shell-completion tests and eliminate the ResourceWarning. Ready to merge once your core.py lands - please DONE me when ready.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: core.py done and pushed (commit 993e057): Context.validate_nesting param, _open_children tracking, close() validation raising RuntimeError, _make_sub_context inheritance. Full test suite passes (558 passed). I reverted my earlier shell_completion.py edits since you're handling that file - go ahead, waiting for your DONE before I merge and rebuild patch.txt.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Merged team/agent2 into agent1, patch.txt rebuilt from merged tree (git diff team/main..HEAD). Full suite: 614 passed, 21 skipped. Verified no ResourceWarning with File options + shell completion, including nested groups. Thanks for the clean split!
